sources: ECB Working Paper Series — on PEPP + APP portfolio rebalancing transmission (TODO: cite specific papers when populated)
Banco de España — Financial Stability Report, QE transmission sections
BIS Quarterly Review articles on unconventional monetary policy
_meta.notes: QE transmission presets — these ratios are the core of the interactive tracer. Each preset answers 'when the ECB prints €X, where does €X land?'. Initial ratios below are ILLUSTRATIVE pedagogical defaults — refine from ECB/BdE working papers and update the as_of + source for each preset. The ordering (bank_reserves largest) reflects the well-documented empirical finding that QE liquidity mostly sits as excess reserves and works via portfolio-rebalancing + yield-compression rather than direct new lending.

presets.label: PEPP · 2020-2022
presets.context: Pandemic Emergency Purchase Programme. Peak QE intensity.
presets.source: TODO: cite specific ECB WP on PEPP transmission
presets.explanation: Most of the printed euros landed as excess reserves at national central banks. The rest transmitted primarily through portfolio rebalancing — allocators rotated into risk assets, pushing up equity + real-estate prices. Fiscal + real-credit channels were comparatively small.

presets.label: APP · 2015-2018
presets.context: Asset Purchase Programme — the original Draghi 'whatever it takes' era QE.
presets.source: TODO: cite specific ECB WP on APP transmission
presets.explanation: Similar dominant pattern — excess reserves expanded massively, asset-price channel was the most material transmission, fiscal channel helped peripheral sovereigns refinance cheaply.

presets.label: Normalisation · 2022-2024
presets.context: QT era. ECB shrinks balance sheet — transmission reversal.
presets.source: TODO: BdE FSR 2024/2 section on QT transmission
presets.explanation: Sign reverses — reserve withdrawal, yield widening, allocator rebalancing back out of risk. Real-economy channel slightly larger because higher rates bite on credit more directly.

bank_reserves.label: Réserves bancaires · BdE
bank_reserves.description: Reserves stay parked at the central bank — no onward transmission. Banks earn the Deposit Facility Rate; they don't lend these reserves out (Werner / BoE 2014).

asset_prices.label: Prix des actifs · allocateurs
asset_prices.description: Portfolio rebalancing — allocators sell bonds to ECB, rotate into equity + real estate, inflate asset prices. Benefits whoever owns assets: pensioners, wealthy households, family offices.

fiscal_channel.label: Canal budgétaire · État
fiscal_channel.description: Compressed sovereign yields let the state refinance cheaply. More fiscal space → spending reaches households + firms via transfers, salaries, procurement.

real_credit.label: Crédit à l'économie · ménages + entreprises
real_credit.description: New lending to real economy. Smallest channel — bank lending is capital-constrained, not reserve-constrained.
